title: Income Tax SFT: High-Value Cash Deposit Reporting
summary: Cash deposits of ₹50 lakh or more in current accounts may be reported under SFT by banks.
content: Under SFT reporting, cash deposits aggregating to ₹50 lakh or more in one or more current accounts of a person during a financial year may be reportable by banks or co-operative banks. Taxpayers and professionals should review high-value cash transactions and maintain proper source documentation. This is a general knowledge update. Please verify applicable law, notification, circular and facts before taking action.
disclaimer: This is a general knowledge update. Please verify applicable law, notification, circular and facts before taking action.

title: ROC: Annual Filing General Timelines
summary: Annual filing (AOC-4 and MGT-7) generally follows specific timelines post-AGM.
content: Annual filing deadlines for financial statements (AOC-4) and annual returns (MGT-7) are generally within 30 and 60 days of the Annual General Meeting (AGM) respectively. Professionals should monitor official Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA) circulars for specific extensions or changes to the filing calendar for the current financial year. Early filing is recommended to avoid technical issues on the portal.
disclaimer: This is a general knowledge update. Please verify applicable law, notification, circular and facts before taking action.

title: Tax Audit: Clause 44 Reconciliation Points
summary: Clause 44 requires reconciliation of GST expenditure details during tax audits.
content:
Clause 44 reporting requires detailed reconciliation of GST-related expenditures. Key focus areas typically include:
1. Matching total expenditure with P&L statements.
2. Correct classification of exempt vs taxable supply expenditures.
3. Identification of expenses from composition dealers.
4. Verification of RCM (Reverse Charge Mechanism) payments.
5. Cross-checking with available GSTR-2B data.

Professionals should ensure that clients maintain robust records to support these classifications.
disclaimer: This is a general knowledge update. Please verify applicable law, notification, circular and facts before taking action.

title: MCA Portal: Troubleshooting DSC & Login Issues
summary: Technical hurdles on the MCA portal can often be resolved with browser and software updates.
content:
Common technical issues during MCA filings can often be resolved through procedural steps:
- Clearing browser cache and using updated browsers for fresh logins.
- Ensuring EmSigner or relevant utility software is updated to the recommended version.
- Following official technical manuals for DSC association and profile updates.
- Utilizing the 'Forgot Password' or 'Reset Profile' utilities if account accessibility issues persist.

Users should refer to the official MCA helpdesk for persistent technical errors.
disclaimer: This is a general knowledge update. Please verify applicable law, notification, circular and facts before taking action.
